bugs.error_message: make audiobook-sample (and all audio targets) failed: 'python: command not found' on macOS Intel i9
bugs.root_cause: Makefile recipes hard-coded `python` while macOS ships only `python3` (no `python` symlink). All 27 audiobook/build python invocations broke on a fresh macOS shell.
bugs.fix: Added `PYTHON ?= python3` near the header; substituted `^\tpython ` -> `\t$(PYTHON) ` across all 27 recipe lines. Override per-call via `make PYTHON=/path/to/venv/bin/python <target>`.

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: Sep should be Nov
Full Ch01 audiobook render failed: kokoro synth failed after 3 retries: APIConnectionError. Container exited cleanly (ExitCode=0, OOMKilled=false), auto-restarted via restart:unless-stopped policy.
bugs.root_cause: CHUNK_CHAR_BUDGET=1400 produces chunks Kokoro CPU image cannot finish under its internal worker timeout. Empirically: a 957-char chunk took 164.9s on this Intel i9 (no GPU); next request after that hit 'peer closed connection without sending complete message body' as the Kokoro worker had restarted. Failure is reproducible at chunk 9/46 of Ch01.
bugs.fix: Two complementary mitigations: (1) lower CHUNK_CHAR_BUDGET from 1400 → ~700 in build/audiobook.py for CPU Kokoro renders; (2) prefer --per-sentence mode for final-quality renders (cleaner prosody per existing audiobook.py comment, smaller per-call payloads). Long-term: add resume/checkpoint to audiobook.py so partial renders survive container restart. Audiobook-sample at 2 paragraphs unaffected because chunks fit under the timeout.

bugs.error_message: Ch01 audiobook render failed at chunk 77/393 even with --per-sentence + CHUNK_CHAR_BUDGET=700. RestartCount=2.
bugs.root_cause: Progressive slowdown in Kokoro CPU image — NOT chunk size. Chunks 1-74 ran ~3-5s each; chunk 75 (50 chars) took 39s, chunk 76 (104 chars) took 35s, chunk 77 hung 60+s and triggered the same APIConnectionError. Pattern matches Python process memory leak, sustained CPU thermal throttle, or Kokoro worker state degradation. Lowering chunk budget did not help — small chunks are also affected once degradation sets in.
bugs.fix: Three options, increasing in cost: (1) PERIODIC RESTART — wrap the render loop to docker restart kokoro-fastapi every ~50 chunks to clear memory; cheapest, but adds ~30s of cold-start overhead per restart. (2) RESUME-CAPABLE RENDER — extend audiobook.py with checkpoint-and-resume so a crashed render picks up where it left off (track completed chunks in a sidecar JSON; concat MP3 segments). Required for any multi-chapter overnight render to be reliable on this hardware. (3) RUN ON THE WINDOWS GPU BOX via Higgs once that's debugged — GPU sustained workload doesn't degrade like CPU does. Or use a paid cloud TTS for final master.

bugs.error_message: GitButler refs stale: workspace ref behind main, target ref pointed at unrelated commit, virtual_branches.toml default_target.sha out of sync
bugs.root_cause: GitButler refs and toml drift over time as origin/main advances; the synthetic 'GitButler Workspace Commit' that the workspace ref points to has parent=stale-main-sha. Target ref had been left at an unrelated commit (6a4dc125, an old epub feature commit). When user has unpushed beacon commits sitting on local main from Yeoman work via GitButler-internal flows, the divergence compounds.
bugs.fix:
1) git fetch origin main. 2) Push every unpushed work branch (polish/yeoman-phase4a-*) and PR-merge to origin. 3) Cherry-pick standalone unpushed beacon commits onto chore branches and PR-merge. 4) git reset --hard origin/main on local main. 5) Edit .git/gitbutler/virtual_branches.toml: set default_target.sha = current origin/main SHA. 6) Create synthetic workspace commit: WS=$(GIT_AUTHOR_NAME=GitButler GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL=gitbutler@gitbutler.com ... git commit-tree $(git rev-parse origin/main^{tree}) -p $(git rev-parse origin/main) -m 'GitButler Workspace Commit

This is placeholder commit and will be replaced by a merge of your virtual branches.

Due to GitButler managing multiple virtual branches, you cannot switch back and'). 7) git update-ref refs/heads/gitbutler/workspace $WS. 8) git update-ref refs/heads/gitbutler/target $(git rev-parse origin/main). 9) Verify: git checkout gitbutler/workspace && but status (must show '[origin/main]' base SHA matching current main). The synthetic commit pattern matches Sunfish's working pattern.
bugs.notes: Second occurrence of this class of issue (first fixed earlier in 2026-05-01 session via the same synthetic-commit pattern). Drift is recurring — each time origin/main advances significantly without GitButler GUI being used to sync, the refs and toml fall behind. GitButler.app running concurrently (PID 77285) does not auto-fix the toml or refs. The fix is safe to apply with the app running; the app picks up the new state after the next refresh.

bugs.error_message: 405 Method Not Allowed on all chatterbox TTS requests — render fails immediately
bugs.root_cause: The Inference Studio proxy at port 8881 routes TTS requests under /api/v1/{path}, not /v1/{path}. The audiobook script's hardcoded default_base_url='http://desktop-umt08rn:8881/v1' and the --base-url passed in the render command both used /v1, so the OpenAI client was hitting /v1/audio/speech which the proxy returns 405 for (the route exists under /api/v1/audio/speech).
bugs.fix: Pass --base-url http://desktop-umt08rn:8881/api/v1 (or /8883/api/v1 for direct backend). The hardcoded default_base_url in the chatterbox engine entry also needs updating to http://desktop-umt08rn:8881/api/v1 to prevent recurrence when --base-url is omitted.

bugs.error_message: 504 Service timeout cascade: chapter-map overrides --preset ciufi-galeazzi to female-solo/broom_salesman; first TTS request triggers proxy 30s timeout, 8 retries flood the server queue (depth 4), subsequent requests get 503 engine queue full
bugs.root_cause: Two issues: (1) CHAPTER_PRESET_MAP has 'book-2/act-1/ch01-departure': 'female-solo' which overrides --preset ciufi-galeazzi unless --no-chapter-map is passed. (2) The reverse proxy at port 8881 has a hard 30-second upstream timeout to port 8883. The ciufi-galeazzi cloned voice inference takes 20-80s per sentence. The 8-retry loop in synth_chunk fires up to 8 new requests before any complete, saturating the 4-slot queue and causing 503s for all subsequent retries.
bugs.fix: Added --no-chapter-map to the render command to bypass chapter-map override. Routed to port 8883 directly (http://desktop-umt08rn:8883/api/v1) to bypass the 30-second proxy timeout. Port 8883 is the direct TTS backend with no proxy timeout restriction.

bugs.error_message: YAML front-matter (title: Departure, volume: 2, etc.) sent to TTS engine as spoken text, causing very slow inference (30+ seconds for a 220-char chunk of metadata key-value pairs)
bugs.root_cause: narratable_text() strips YAML front-matter delimiter lines (---) via re.sub(r'(?m)^\s*---+\s*$', '', t) but leaves the content between the delimiters (title:, volume:, act:, icm-stage:, etc.) as plain text. Sentence chunker then presents this metadata as chunk 2 to the TTS engine.
bugs.fix:
Added YAML front-matter strip at the start of narratable_text(): re.sub(r'\A---
.*?
---
', '', t, flags=re.DOTALL). Added ISO 8601 timestamp expansion (2026-09-15T11:18 → September 15, 2026, at 11:18) to prevent slow phonemization of date-time strings.

bugs.error_message: higgs synth failed after 8 retries on chunk 90/270 of ch01-departure. Reproduced in two consecutive runs (run 1: 7x APITimeoutError + 1x 503 engine queue full; run 2: 8x APITimeoutError, server then fully unresponsive HTTP 000).
bugs.file: build/audiobook.py (server-side defect: chatterbox-tts on desktop-umt08rn, ciufi-galeazzi voice)
bugs.root_cause: DETERMINISTIC POISON CHUNK at index 90 of ch01-departure (per-sentence mode, ciufi-galeazzi cloned voice). Both runs froze on the same chunk with identical 8/8 retry sequence. The wedged inference worker eventually fills the request queue (503) and then takes the entire endpoint offline (HTTP 000). Self-recovery (queue drain + cache-resume restart) DOES NOT WORK — the same chunk re-poisons the worker. Likely trigger sentences are around script positions 88-92: 'The press conference yesterday had taken the morning.' / 'Stefan had been at the Bremerhaven dock to see his boat off.' / 'I had not been there.' / 'I had read the press cycle from St.' — all short, ordinary text. Trigger may be specific to a voice/punctuation/tokenizer interaction (note 'St.' producing a 1-char fragment after sentence-split, which could exercise an edge case).
bugs.fix: Two-step intervention required, BOTH steps needed: (1) Restart chatterbox-tts service on the Windows GPU box (desktop-umt08rn) — server is currently unresponsive, queue full, worker wedged. (2) Then EITHER: (a) bisect chunk 90's text — likely 'I had read the press cycle from St.' the fragmenting period after 'St.' is a strong suspect, or (b) try a different preset (e.g. female-solo) to test if it is voice-specific, or (c) bump per-request timeout in audiobook.py synth_chunk to give the inference call longer, or (d) add chunk-skip-on-fail mode in audiobook.py that records the failed chunk index and continues, leaving a silent gap to repair manually with --max-paragraphs targeting just that range. Cache-only resume is INSUFFICIENT — chunk 90 must be made synthesizable or skipped.

bugs.error_message: Audiobook listener hears chapter-opening log-block boilerplate before main prose (CO directive 2026-05-07)
bugs.root_cause: narratable_text() bug-191 fix stripped only the YAML front-matter --- block. Vol 2 chapters with log-opener-pattern: A also have an italicized 'filed log' artifact between YAML and main prose (e.g., Ch 1 'Pre-departure record, dated...' + 'Crew complement of nine...' + '— Filed to mission-archive; hash 7c41...e0b9'), bookended below by a horizontal-rule. Print reader experiences this as a deliberate craft frame; audio listener experiences ~90 seconds of bureaucratic boilerplate before the story starts.
bugs.fix: Added a second regex strip in narratable_text() after the YAML strip: re.sub(r'\\A(?:\\s*\\*[^*]+\\*)+\\s*---\\s*\\n', '', t). Anchored at \\A so it only fires at the start of post-YAML text; will not match in-prose italicized passages later in chapters. Verified working on Ch 1, Ch 5, Ch 13 — all three drop straight into Anna's narration.

bugs.error_message: APITimeoutError on chunk 79/258 of ch01-departure: TTS /audio/speech endpoint hangs; voices endpoint returns 200 but inference does not respond
bugs.root_cause: Chatterbox inference worker on GPU box is hung — API gateway (port 8883) accepts connections and returns 200 on /voices but does not process /audio/speech inference requests. First 78 chunks completed from cache; chunk 79 is the first fresh-inference request. Simple 'Hello world.' test also times out at 120s confirming inference is stuck, not chunk content. Not a poison-chunk (content issue) — server-side inference worker failure.
bugs.fix: Render was killed after 2x 300s timeouts on chunk 79. Server must be restarted (restart Chatterbox/higgs inference worker on Windows GPU box) before re-running the render. No code fix needed; the render script and audiobook.py are correct.

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught
No early return/throw for edge case: !st || st === 'None'

bugs.error_message: Text-audio highlighting completely broken after ch01-departure audio regeneration — no paragraphs highlighted
bugs.root_cause: Regenerated alignment JSON files have source_text: null serialized as the literal Python string 'None' instead of null/empty. buildChunkMap() calls scoreMatch(el.textContent, chunk.source_text) — normText('None') = 'none', which never prefix-matches any paragraph, so bestScore never reaches 8 and every chunk maps to element: null.
bugs.fix: Added chunkMatchText(chunk) helper that returns chunk.text when source_text is falsy or the literal string 'None'. Used chunkMatchText(chunk) in both scoreMatch call (line 56) and chNorm computation (line 67) inside buildChunkMap. chunk.text contains clean TTS-processed prose that matches DOM content for narrative Vol-2 chapters.

bugs.error_message: Highlighting stops after first paragraph; no sentence/word highlighting visible
bugs.root_cause: scoreMatch() had the substring fallback in only one direction: took element text snippet and searched within chunk text. This handled the legacy YAML-frontmatter case (large source_text, element appears mid-chunk) but completely failed for per-sentence audio where each chunk IS one sentence. Sentences 2+ of a multi-sentence paragraph could never match their paragraph element — they matched nothing, element=null, clearing the highlight immediately.
bugs.fix: Added bidirectional matching in scoreMatch: Case 1 prefix, Case 2 chunk-in-element (take first 20 chars of chunk text, search within element — handles per-sentence audio), Case 3 element-in-chunk (original YAML frontmatter case). Match rate on ch01-departure went from ~23% to 97.7%.

bugs.root_cause: Property access on potentially null/undefined value

bugs.error_message: aeneas tail-compression: 69/257 spoken chunks at ch01-departure end have start=end=1430.8s (zero duration), producing empty words[] arrays
bugs.root_cause: aeneas DTW algorithm compresses unresolved tail fragments to the last valid audio timestamp when it loses confidence near the end of long audio. 188/257 chunks aligned correctly; remaining 69 pile at the final timestamp with zero duration. Known aeneas behavior on audio >20 min.
bugs.fix: Not a bug in our code — expected aeneas behavior. merge_alignment.py correctly sets words=[] for zero-duration chunks. Web reader falls back to linear interpolation for those chunks. For v2 improvement: tune aeneas MFCC window size for long audio, or split long chapters into segments before alignment.

bugs.error_message: POST /api/queue immediately started rendering; no way to stage items before processing
bugs.root_cause: POST /api/queue called processNextInQueue() immediately, making it impossible to batch-stage then explicitly trigger processing
bugs.fix: Added stagedQueue[] separate from jobQueue[]; POST /api/queue pushes to stagedQueue (status: staged); POST /api/queue/process moves staged→pending and starts processNextInQueue(); batchTotal/batchDone track progress for footer bar

bugs.error_message: PATCH /api/review/comment/:idx returns Cannot PATCH (404) — save button appears non-functional
bugs.root_cause: Server process was started before PATCH endpoint was added to server.js. LaunchAgent runs node server.js from disk but process keeps old in-memory routes until restarted. Empty catch{} in handleSaveEditNote swallowed the 404 silently.
bugs.fix: Restarted LaunchAgent via launchctl kickstart -k. Added r.ok check + console.error to handleSaveEditNote so future failures are visible rather than silent.
